behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Identifying 'all key terms' depends entirely on LLM semantic understanding. A mock extractor could always claim success, hiding real failures where the LLM misses terms phrased indirectly, using synonyms, or embedded in complex sentences. The real failure boundary is whether the production LLM successfully recognizes terms across linguistic variations.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test contract with known key terms (parties, specific dates, defined obligations, named penalties). Call the real clause extractor. Verify it identifies all expected key terms, including when they use different phrasings, pronouns, or indirect references. Include cases with compound sentences and embedded clauses.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: The phrase 'even when...indirect or euphemistic' is the key — this requires semantic understanding that a keyword list or regex cannot provide. Mocking the risk assessor hides the real failure: does the production LLM actually understand nuance and indirect language? A mock would return whatever we hardcoded, not what a real LLM produces.
behaviors.test_description: Create test contracts with both explicit problematic language (e.g., 'unlimited liability') and euphemistic variants (e.g., 'unrestricted exposure', 'full indemnification'). Call the real risk assessor. Verify it flags all variants with appropriate risk severity levels, including borderline cases that require judgment.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Regulatory compliance is jurisdiction-specific and requires domain knowledge (e.g., California CCPA requirements differ from EU GDPR). A mocked compliance checker cannot verify that the real LLM knows the regulations and applies them correctly. Real failures occur when the LLM misinterprets regulations or lacks knowledge about a jurisdiction's requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Create test contracts for multiple jurisdictions (California, EU, Texas, etc.) with varying compliance gaps — some fully compliant, some partially, some clearly non-compliant. Call the real compliance checker for each. Verify it correctly identifies applicable requirements for that jurisdiction and accurately assesses compliance status.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says 'standard' but risk assessor says 'high risk'), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: The merge step must correctly identify which assessments refer to 'the same clause' and preserve both with clear attribution. While the merge logic could be tested with simple unit inputs, real failures emerge when merge must handle the actual output structures from the three reviewers — how they reference clauses, format assessments, and encode metadata. Integration testing with realistic reviewer outputs (mocked or test data) catches clause-matching bugs and format assumptions that unit tests miss.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three reviewers to return realistic, structured outputs where two reviewers assess the same clause with conflicting conclusions (e.g., extractor labels it as 'obligation', risk assessor labels it as 'high_risk'). Call the merge step. Verify the merged report includes both assessments under the same clause with clear source attribution, allowing tracing back to the originating reviewer.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end orchestration test requiring multiple real LLM calls, actual parallel execution, and completeness verification. Real failures include: (1) one reviewer times out and results are lost, (2) parallel dispatch isn't actually concurrent, (3) merge fails on real, complex LLM outputs, (4) error handling drops findings when one reviewer fails. Only a Workflow test with real LLM calls orchestrated in parallel can catch these failures. Unit/Integration tests with mocked LLMs would hide correctness issues; System tests without real LLMs would hide semantic failures.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real, multi-clause contract to the full pipeline. Execute parallel dispatch to all three reviewers and collect results. Verify the merged report contains all findings from all three reviewers with no loss. Test error cases where one reviewer is slow or temporarily fails, verifying the system gracefully collects results from the others and produces a complete merged report.
